expectations.text: Identifies that t3 conflicts with both t1 and t2 since whole-file claim includes all sections
expectations.evidence: Pairwise conflict table shows t1 vs t3 and t2 vs t3 both marked YES with rule 'Whole-file includes section'. Resolution text states 't3 conflicts with both t1 and t2'.

expectations.text: Recognizes that t1 and t2 do NOT conflict since they write different sections
expectations.evidence: Pairwise conflict table shows t1 vs t2 marked NO with rule 'Different sections'. Paths are config.yml#header vs config.yml#footer.

expectations.text: Places t1 and t2 together in one wave as parallel
expectations.evidence: Wave 1 contains tasks ['t1', 't2'] with parallel: true. Rationale states 't1 and t2 write different sections of config.yml (#header vs #footer) — no overlap, parallel-safe.'

expectations.text: Moves t3 to a separate wave from t1 and t2
expectations.evidence: Wave 2 contains tasks ['t3'] with depends_on_waves: [1]. Resolution explicitly states 't3 moves to Wave 2.' Final state confirms Wave 1: [t1, t2], Wave 2: [t3].

Multiple explanations provided: (1) conflict table uses rule 'Different sections' for t1/t2 and 'Whole-file includes section' for t3 pairs, (2) wave 1 rationale explains section-level isolation, (3) wave 2 rationale explains 't3 writes whole config.yml, conflicting with both t1 (#header) and t2 (#footer)', (4) tie-break logic is explained with downstream dependents and task index ordering.
